core_question: Should we push the 2 clean commits to origin/main now, publishing this session's XP overhaul to the live portfolio?
knowns: Working tree is clean; 2 commits (51b2b13 scaffold, 0227809 feat) ahead of origin/main after soft-reset + recommit
cs225sp26 course PDFs were removed from both working tree and staged index before recommit, so they never reach origin
Last `npm run build` on the exact HEAD we are pushing passed clean — 7 static pages exported, CSS hash 451710133516a162
No force-push required: origin/main has never seen the collapsed 8 checkpoint commits, so a regular push is sufficient
GitHub Pages deploys on push to main via GitHub Actions; a broken build there prevents the live site from updating rather than breaking it
All code paths touched (Minesweeper, resize, explorer polish, mobile scroll, boot flow) live in the XP desktop shell; SEO pages were smoke-built but not behaviorally changed
unknowns: If the GitHub Actions build step exits non-zero within 5 minutes after push, the deploy fails and the Actions tab shows a red X — root cause is likely a Node major mismatch between local 20.14 and CI
When iOS Safari crosses the sticky-taskbar viewport seam, if scroll jitter exceeds 8 px per frame during a 5 seconds scroll test, the sticky rule regresses on real hardware
If 5 consecutive finger taps against the 4 px edge-resize strip produce 0 successful resize gestures, the touch target fails usability and mobile users cannot resize windows
assumptions: GitHub Actions deploy workflow is wired and hasn't rotted since the last successful deploy
Static export output on CI matches local output because Next.js static export is deterministic given identical inputs and Node majors
Removing PDFs that were only in local checkpoint commits has zero downstream impact because no code or markdown referenced them
Rolling back after push is cheap: `git revert <sha>` and redeploy take under 2 minutes each
disconfirmation: If the GitHub Actions deploy workflow exits non-zero within 5 minutes of this push, or if Minesweeper fails to open on the live site after deploy completes, revert commit 0227809 and redeploy
